column1: Always prioritize clinic flow if unsure of what to do. Rooming flow is the first priority. Maintain at least a 1 roomer : 2 provider ratio unless it is mathematically impossible. MAs are the default roomers. RNs can room patients. Some MA/PSR cross-trained staff can room only when needed to preserve the minimum rooming ratio. If no PSR is assigned
column2: the Office Manager acts as PSR and protects check in/out first. If help is needed
column3: the person needing help should ask directly
column5: and verify the request was heard. If there is a single PSR
column6: roomers should help with checkout/scheduling when it does not break rooming flow. Definitions: Rooming means everything from the beginning of rooming until the patient is ready for checkout
column7: including nurse visits when assigned. Lower-priority work such as forms
column11: and duty lists should wait until rooming
